req.title: Interest accrual (boundary variant)
req.statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-041 mandates daily tier-rate interest accrual credited on the last business day of the month via /cron/interest. Four test cases cover: (1) EP positive – a mid-tier balance accrues and credits correctly; (2) NEG negative – a reconciliation variance is reported when ledger totals mismatch; (3) BVA boundary-min – a balance exactly at the minimum tier threshold accrues at the lowest tier rate; (4) BVA boundary-max – a balance exactly at the maximum tier threshold accrues at the highest tier rate without overflow. Each test seeds its own account, triggers the cron endpoint, then tears down, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_interest_credited_standard_tier_balance
preconditions: Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
A savings account with a mid-tier balance (e.g. 5000.00) exists and has accrued daily interest for the full month
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: Seed a savings account via <fixture: seed_account> with balance 5000.00
Simulate N daily accrual records via <helper_fn: seed_daily_accruals>
POST /cron/interest with valid cron auth header
GET /accounts/{account_id}/transactions to fetch ledger entries
DELETE seeded account via <helper_fn: teardown_account>
expected: A ledger entry of type 'INTEREST_CREDIT' exists for the account
Credited amount equals sum of daily accruals rounded to 2 decimal places
trace: R-FIN-041: interest credited monthly on last business day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
CRON_AUTH = {"X-Cron-Secret": "<cron_secret>"}

def test_interest_credited_standard_tier_balance():
    account_id, tier_rate = <helper_fn: seed_account>(balance="5000.00", tier="MID")
    days_in_month = 30
    expected = (Decimal("5000.00") * Decimal(str(tier_rate)) / 365 * days_in_month).quantize(
        Decimal("0.01"), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP
    )
    <helper_fn: seed_daily_accruals>(account_id, days=days_in_month)
    try:
        r = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/interest", headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10)
        assert r.status_code == 200
        body = r.json()
        assert body.get("credited_count", 0) >= 1
        txns = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{account_id}/transactions",
            headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10
        ).json()
        credits = [t for t in txns if t["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT"]
        assert len(credits) == 1
        actual = Decimal(str(credits[0]["amount"]))
        assert actual == expected
    finally:
        <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account_id)

tests.name: test_reconciliation_variance_reported_on_ledger_mismatch
preconditions: Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
A savings account exists whose accrual records have been tampered to introduce a variance
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: Seed a savings account via <fixture: seed_account> with balance 3000.00
Seed corrupted daily accruals with an intentional variance via <helper_fn: seed_corrupt_accruals>
POST /cron/interest with valid cron auth header
GET /reconciliation/variances to fetch variance report
DELETE seeded account via <helper_fn: teardown_account>
expected: Response body contains 'variance_count' >= 1 OR 'errors' list is non-empty
GET /reconciliation/variances returns at least one entry referencing the account_id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
CRON_AUTH = {"X-Cron-Secret": "<cron_secret>"}

def test_reconciliation_variance_reported_on_ledger_mismatch():
    account_id = <helper_fn: seed_account>(balance="3000.00", tier="MID")
    <helper_fn: seed_corrupt_accruals>(account_id, corrupt_delta="9999.99")
    try:
        r = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/interest", headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10)
        assert r.status_code in (200, 207)
        body = r.json()
        has_variance = (
            body.get("variance_count", 0) >= 1
            or len(body.get("errors", [])) >= 1
        )
        assert has_variance, f"Expected variance signal, got: {body}"
        variances = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/reconciliation/variances",
            headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10
        ).json()
        account_variances = [v for v in variances if v.get("account_id") == account_id]
        assert len(account_variances) >= 1
        v = account_variances[0]
        assert "expected_amount" in v and "actual_amount" in v
    finally:
        <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account_id)

tests.name: test_interest_accrual_at_minimum_tier_boundary
preconditions: Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
A savings account exists with balance exactly equal to the minimum tier threshold
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: Seed a savings account with balance exactly MIN_TIER_THRESHOLD via <fixture: seed_account>
Seed daily accruals for 30 days using the minimum tier rate via <helper_fn: seed_daily_accruals>
POST /cron/interest with valid cron auth header
GET /accounts/{account_id}/transactions and filter for INTEREST_CREDIT
DELETE seeded account via <helper_fn: teardown_account>
expected: Exactly one INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry exists for the account
Credited amount equals floor calculation of MIN_TIER_RATE applied to MIN_TIER_THRESHOLD for 30 days
No overdraft or zero-credit ledger entry is created
trace: R-FIN-041: boundary behaviour at exact min threshold must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
CRON_AUTH = {"X-Cron-Secret": "<cron_secret>"}
MIN_TIER_THRESHOLD = Decimal("1.00")
MIN_TIER_RATE = Decimal("<min_tier_rate>")

def test_interest_accrual_at_minimum_tier_boundary():
    account_id = <helper_fn: seed_account>(
        balance=str(MIN_TIER_THRESHOLD), tier="MIN"
    )
    days = 30
    expected = (
        MIN_TIER_THRESHOLD * MIN_TIER_RATE / 365 * days
    ).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)
    <helper_fn: seed_daily_accruals>(account_id, days=days)
    try:
        r = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/interest", headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10)
        assert r.status_code == 200
        txns = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{account_id}/transactions",
            headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10
        ).json()
        credits = [t for t in txns if t["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT"]
        assert len(credits) == 1, f"Expected 1 credit entry, got {len(credits)}"
        actual = Decimal(str(credits[0]["amount"]))
        assert actual == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {actual}"
        assert actual > Decimal("0.00"), "Interest must be positive at min boundary"
    finally:
        <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account_id)

tests.name: test_interest_accrual_at_maximum_tier_boundary
preconditions: Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
A savings account exists with balance exactly equal to the maximum tier threshold
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: Seed a savings account with balance exactly MAX_TIER_THRESHOLD via <fixture: seed_account>
Seed daily accruals for 31 days using the maximum tier rate via <helper_fn: seed_daily_accruals>
POST /cron/interest with valid cron auth header
GET /accounts/{account_id}/transactions and filter for INTEREST_CREDIT
DELETE seeded account via <helper_fn: teardown_account>
expected: Exactly one INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry exists for the account
Credited amount equals calculation of MAX_TIER_RATE applied to MAX_TIER_THRESHOLD for 31 days without numeric overflow
No reconciliation variance entry is created for this account
trace: R-FIN-041: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
CRON_AUTH = {"X-Cron-Secret": "<cron_secret>"}
MAX_TIER_THRESHOLD = Decimal("999999.99")
MAX_TIER_RATE = Decimal("<max_tier_rate>")

def test_interest_accrual_at_maximum_tier_boundary():
    account_id = <helper_fn: seed_account>(
        balance=str(MAX_TIER_THRESHOLD), tier="MAX"
    )
    days = 31
    expected = (
        MAX_TIER_THRESHOLD * MAX_TIER_RATE / 365 * days
    ).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)
    <helper_fn: seed_daily_accruals>(account_id, days=days)
    try:
        r = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/interest", headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10)
        assert r.status_code == 200
        txns = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{account_id}/transactions",
            headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10
        ).json()
        credits = [t for t in txns if t["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT"]
        assert len(credits) == 1, f"Expected 1 credit entry, got {len(credits)}"
        actual = Decimal(str(credits[0]["amount"]))
        assert actual == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {actual}"
        variances = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/reconciliation/variances",
            headers=CRON_AUTH, timeout=10
        ).json()
        assert not any(v.get("account_id") == account_id for v in variances)
    finally:
        <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account_id)
